[2022-07-25 17:29] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In terms of how you defend, those are the only two options
[2022-07-25 17:32] lyra1712 (Lyra): well, theres also gambiting
[2022-07-25 17:32] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I guess something worth considering is how you interact with absorb
[2022-07-25 17:32] lyra1712 (Lyra): not a strategy per se, but worth mentioning
[2022-07-25 17:32] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): There are some sets where some combos enable worthwhile absorb denial
[2022-07-25 17:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Like syncing isos vs a big defender in absence of other good attack
[2022-07-25 17:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Or spamming thermites to delay giving opponent absorb, though this is rarely the best play I believe
[2022-07-25 17:35] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And yea as Lyra said, gambiting is when opponent has to expend some amount of resources or opportunity cost in order to realize their full attack threat, so depending on your board you might choose to 'gambit' the defense, allowing them to breach (you get no absorb, and if you gambited for more than exact then they even get to kill things post-breach) in exchange for them expending their threat resource
[2022-07-25 17:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but anyway, as was mentioned before, Prismata is less about strategies that you go for, and more about the strongest combination of units in a given set
[2022-07-25 17:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): some sets might be difficult to read and have multiple viable unit combinations
[2022-07-25 17:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but you don't start queuing ranked thinking you will play breachproof
[2022-07-25 17:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): it's just that in some sets, the strongest combo of units is the bp units
[2022-07-25 17:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): An interesting variant of BP is Venge all-in
[2022-07-25 17:41] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I guess Venge cannon is the only unit that makes some sets BP even on its own without any other BP unit (?)
[2022-07-25 17:41] .sha6001 (Sha): So Pristama only replay value is its second set?
[2022-07-25 17:43] amalloy: in the same sense that sudoku's only replay value is that you get different clues every time, i guess? like yes, that variety is the whole point
[2022-07-25 17:43] .sha6001 (Sha): Before becoming a top player, you need to master most pristama strats
[2022-07-25 17:43] .sha6001 (Sha): and improve your set reading I guess
[2022-07-25 17:44] .sha6001 (Sha): Can you force a opponent to lose with meta gameplay?
[2022-07-25 17:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I'd say you need to master attack/defense nuance, and set reading is an "infinitely" long learning experience
[2022-07-25 17:44] amalloy: unplug their router?
[2022-07-25 17:44] .sha6001 (Sha): not that kind of meta game play
[2022-07-25 17:45] .sha6001 (Sha): like pretending to go in one strat and then switching to another
[2022-07-25 17:45] amalloy: the question makes no sense. you force them to lose by making the best plays
[2022-07-25 17:45] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): There are some sets where the best play can be to just "posture" without "committing" to a particular tech
[2022-07-25 17:46] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And delay committal until a key turning point
[2022-07-25 17:46] .sha6001 (Sha): there is a point where both players play each and every turn perfectly
[2022-07-25 17:47] awaclus: yeah because sometimes there is a counter to everything that you could possibly do, so as soon as you commit, you get countered and lose, so the optimal strategy is for neither player to ever commit
[2022-07-25 17:47] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): There can be some sets like that, but more often games have enough depth that the better player wins
[2022-07-25 17:47] lyra1712 (Lyra): how would you answer that question if it was about chess ?
[2022-07-25 17:47] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Unless you are talking two perfect players in theory
[2022-07-25 17:48] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): With two perfect players in theory, some sets are definitely a forced win, others can be a forced draw (maybe)
[2022-07-25 17:48] awaclus: like, if you're doing something that seems like you're going for a strat that doesn't actually commit to that strat, and then your opponent commits to countering that strat, your opponent just made a huge mistake
[2022-07-25 17:51] awaclus: and I guess some games I have played have played out like that, with e.g. one player thinking the other's third engi means there's enough time to build a strat that dies to aggression and commits to that, and then the third engi player just wastes their third engi and starts playing aggressively and wins
[2022-07-25 17:54] awaclus: but in that case, it's not so much the case that the third engi player is pretending to go for a strat in order to trick the opponent, it's more the case that the third engi player is going for a move that gives enough room to respond aggressively if that becomes necessary and to build slower otherwise, and the opponent is misjudging the situation
[2022-07-25 17:55] .sha6001 (Sha): You can also do the thing called "seeing the future"
[2022-07-25 17:56] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): did it predict lottery numbers for you?
[2022-07-25 17:57] .sha6001 (Sha): Nope, master bots just buys a ton of tickets
[2022-07-25 21:54] sano712 (sano): You can pretend to go for the queen's gambit, but actually go for the queen gambit
[2022-07-25 21:54] sano712 (sano): That's my favourite strat

[2022-07-26 15:34] jamberine: sometimes breachproof is the best strat and you go breachproof yourself
[2022-07-26 15:34] jamberine: otherwise you basically do the same thing you usually do
[2022-07-26 15:35] jamberine: you can value attack a little less after they go breachproof (the whole point of being breachproof is that the health of your units is more efficient than soak)
[2022-07-26 15:35] jamberine: though before they go breachproof if they're not buying an absorber you can sometimes value attack more at that point
[2022-07-26 15:36] jamberine: try to get them to buy forcefields(usually) or go bp earlier than they would like
[2022-07-26 15:40] jamberine: also the economy units tend to be less breachproof than the attackers
[2022-07-26 15:40] jamberine: it can still be worth hitting the attackers if there's not that much supply or you just need to defend, but otherwise hit the money

[2022-08-30 09:44] velizar_ (Name): When animus screws you with its lack of granularity but it's a good deal anyway and the game is too fast for you to want to buy the drones to spend the wasted red, e.g. if you get to build a shadowfang and waste 1 red, than is a highly efficient attacker compared to most other attackers in the game.
[2022-08-30 09:46] velizar_ (Name): When you really want to buy Vai, e.g. your opponent has D.Grid, but you can't spend all of the red afterwards, it's still worth doing.
[2022-08-30 09:48] velizar_ (Name): Sometimes getting that production enables you to do something very efficient, but then you have to waste resources, but not much, so overall it's a good deal, for example if you buy an Animus to buy Cynestra, but after buying it you waste some of the red in order to buy defense or conduits, then you buy more Cynestras...
[2022-08-30 09:51] velizar_ (Name): I've seen top players waste Red/Blue, it's not rare for them, so it's probably often worth wasting.

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: When animus screws you with its lack of granularity but it's a good deal anyway and the game is too fast for you to want to buy the drones to spend the wasted red, e.g. if you get to build a shadowfang and waste 1 red, than is a highly efficient attacker compared to most other attackers in the game.

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: When you really want to buy Vai, e.g. your opponent has D.Grid, but you can't spend all of the red afterwards, it's still worth doing.

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: Sometimes getting that production enables you to do something very efficient, but then you have to waste resources, but not much, so overall it's a good deal, for example if you buy an Animus to buy Cynestra, but after buying it you waste some of the red in order to buy defense or conduits, then you buy more Cynestras...

[2023-02-08 16:49] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): fXc5z-QAEIC what was my mistake, I only spotted one where I could've killed a drone and I dind't but other wise I thought I was ahead on tempo all game?
[2023-02-08 16:52] awaclus: your biggest mistakes were floating 4 gold on turn 2 and buying two Animuss
[2023-02-08 16:54] awaclus: a second Animus is generally only reasonable if there is a unit that costs 3 or more red or a unit with a click ability that costs red, plus in very rare extremely high econ situations
[2023-02-08 16:56] awaclus: DD/DDC, DD/DDE and DD/DA would all have been justifiable on turn 2 there as player 2, but DD/DA seems the strongest to me
[2023-02-08 17:00] awaclus: in this game you really need blue because there isn't a fast enough rush available, so you need access to Wall, and in order to efficiently spend four red and a blue, you need a minimum of 17 Drones, which is assuming you're building four Immolites which is not what you can actually do since you need to build more defense than just one Wall per turn
[2023-02-08 17:01] awaclus: 17 Drones is not beyond ridiculous here because Shredder is a good defender, but Bloodrager makes high econ a lot worse
[2023-02-08 17:02] awaclus: so a lower number of drones and tech is probably good
[2023-02-08 17:02] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): dman, I thought those red guys I bought were good pressure, I wanted to get them out ealier and scare opp
[2023-02-08 17:02] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): did I really float 4 gold?
[2023-02-08 17:02] awaclus: yeah, you went DD/DD
[2023-02-08 17:03] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): oh yeah, I didn't want to go DDE
[2023-02-08 17:03] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): I thouht DD DD was standard
[2023-02-08 17:03] awaclus: standard for p2 is either DA or DDC
[2023-02-08 17:03] awaclus: (or DDE but you already decided you didn't want that, which in this set seems good)
[2023-02-08 17:04] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): seems good as in I should've?
[2023-02-08 17:04] awaclus: no, seems good to decide not to
[2023-02-08 17:05] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): I was debating da but  thought that would stall my econ
[2023-02-08 17:06] awaclus: it does put you into a slightly inconvenient spot econ-wise, but it puts enough pressure on the opponent that it's usually fine

[2023-02-08 17:53] awaclus: one pretty obvious mistake is on turn 7 when you're taking 3 damage and you assign it to a rhino and absorb 1 on a wall, when you could have assigned it to an engineer to absorb 2 instead
[2023-02-08 17:56] awaclus: the Rhino is especially important there because it's your third attack, so it lets you buy Bloodrager to make the opponent's Polywall a massive waste of tempo, but without the Rhino, you ended up getting your other 2 damage absorbed
[2023-02-08 17:57] awaclus: in general, a couple of times you bought drones and floated tech, even though you already had enough drones to efficiently use your tech buildings
[2023-02-08 17:57] awaclus: which is inefficient
[2023-02-08 17:58] awaclus: it's not necessarily *always* bad to waste tech to build drones, but unless you have a solid reason to do it, it's probably better not to
[2023-02-08 18:00] awaclus: also, Hannibull is a great unit here because the existence of Bloodrager means that your opponent is threatening to deal more damage than they're actually going to deal
[2023-02-08 18:00] awaclus: if you use Hannibull as a blocker, it deals with the threat but still also deals damage
[2023-02-08 18:01] awaclus: and later on when the opponent has a massive attack from all the Bloodragers they've been building, the fact that it soaks 7 damage comes in handy
[2023-02-08 18:01] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): yeah, I'm looking at it now. I think my econ brain went brrr that game
[2023-02-08 18:04] awaclus: I'm not sure if Zemora is good enough here, but it's also worth considering because Polywall really helps it (but enemy Bloodragers are annoying to deal with, possibly annoying enough to make it not viable)
[2023-02-08 18:10] awaclus: Master Bot is beatable with Zemora at least but it was a bit too close of a matchup, probably not viable against a human opponent
[2023-02-08 18:11] awaclus: (or you just need to play the Zemora line a lot better than I just did against the bot, I guess)
